title: DNS Tunnelling - Covert C2 Channel via DNS TXT Queries
raw_evidence: 2025-07-14T18:07:44Z — DNS analytics alert: WS-LEGAL-199 generated 9,847 DNS TXT queries in 6 hours — 312x above the host baseline of 31 queries/day.
2025-07-14T18:07:45Z — Query pattern: subdomains follow base32-encoded format (e.g. MFRA2YLNMFXGI.tunnel-c2.xyz) — consistent with DNS tunnelling.
2025-07-14T18:07:47Z — Destination domain tunnel-c2.xyz registered 11 days ago — no legitimate business relationship.
2025-07-14T18:07:50Z — TXT response payloads average 180 bytes — significantly above normal TXT record size; consistent with data encoding.
2025-07-14T18:07:55Z — No direct internet access from this host — all DNS routed via internal resolver then to 8.8.8.8. HTTP/HTTPS proxy logs show no anomaly (tunnelling bypasses proxy).
2025-07-14T18:08:03Z — Process generating queries: svchost.exe (PID 4812) — unusual; legitimate svchost rarely generates bulk DNS TXT queries.
2025-07-14T18:09:12Z — WS-LEGAL-199 hosts active M&A due diligence materials for a pending acquisition (highly sensitive).

context.host_sensitivity: CRITICAL — active M&A due diligence documents present on host
